seoTitle: Anthony vs Apple Tree Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Anthony vs Apple Tree Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Anthony and Apple Tree Creek.
heading: Anthony vs Apple Tree Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Anthony, Queensland and Apple Tree Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Anthony has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Apple Tree Creek (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Anthony or Apple Tree Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Anthony has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Apple Tree Creek scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Anthony scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Apple Tree Creek scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Anthony or Apple Tree Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Anthony has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
